title: Number of Connected Components in an Undirected Graph
description: Given an undirected graph represented as an array of edges, where each edge is a pair of vertices, determine the number of connected components in the graph. A connected component is a subset of the graph where there is a path between any two vertices in this subset.

examples.explanation: There are two connected components: one containing vertices 1, 2, and 3, and another containing vertices 4 and 5.
The graph has two connected components: {1, 2} and {3, 4}, thus the total count of connected components is 2.
An empty graph with no edges has no connected components, resulting in a count of 0.

constraints: The number of edges will be between 0 and 1000.
Each edge is represented by two distinct integers ranging from 1 to n, where n is the number of vertices.
hints: Consider using Depth-First Search or Breadth-First Search to explore the graph.
You may want to keep track of visited nodes to avoid counting them multiple times.
Think about how you can represent the graph using an adjacency list or matrix.
Union-Find can also be a useful approach to find connected components efficiently.
